text.title: Laravel Enterprise Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the enterprise Laravel 10/11 patterns. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST consume `$request->validated()` from a FormRequest. `$request->all()`, `$request->input()` for protected fields, and `Model::create($request->all())` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: `all()` lets a client inject any field (`is_admin`, `password_hash`); the FormRequest contract is the only source of trusted input.

rule.statement: Multi-step writes MUST be wrapped in `DB::transaction(fn() => ...)`. Side effects (mail, queue dispatch) MUST run after commit via `DB::afterCommit`.
rule.rationale: Without a transaction, a partial failure leaves the database inconsistent; mail inside the transaction sends even when the row rolls back.

rule.statement: Any iteration over an Eloquent collection that touches a relation MUST eager-load that relation via `->with(...)`. `Model::preventLazyLoading()` MUST be enabled in non-prod environments.
rule.rationale: N+1 is the most common Laravel perf bug; `preventLazyLoading` fails loudly in dev so it never ships.

rule.statement: API Resources MUST list every attribute explicitly. `$this->toArray()`, `$this->all()`, and spread (`...$this->attributes`) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit lists leak `password`, `remember_token`, soft-delete columns when the model grows new fields.

rule.statement: Queue jobs that mutate external state MUST implement `ShouldBeUnique` and declare a `uniqueId()` based on the work payload. Idempotency MUST be testable.
rule.rationale: Workers retry on failure; non-idempotent jobs double-charge customers, send duplicate emails, or corrupt rows.

rule.statement: Services / Actions MUST receive dependencies via constructor injection. `app(Service::class)` and `resolve(Service::class)` are forbidden outside container bindings and tests.
rule.rationale: Container resolution inside code bypasses autowiring and prevents mocks in tests.
